codeReview.summary: The feature implements core intake classification and session creation, but it contains a blocking security/validation bug in domain matching that accepts non-Douyin hostnames containing 'douyin.com' as a substring, violating supported-input constraints.

issues.description: Hostname validation uses `hostname.includes('douyin.com')`, which incorrectly accepts attacker-controlled domains like `douyin.com.evil.com` or `notdouyin.com`. This can misclassify unsupported links as supported and create sessions for invalid domains. Validation must require exact approved hosts (e.g., `www.douyin.com`, `douyin.com`, `v.douyin.com`) instead of substring matching.

Classification tests do not cover deceptive hostname edge cases (e.g., `https://douyin.com.evil.com/video/123`, `https://notdouyin.com/video/123`), which allowed the domain-validation bug to pass undetected.

sharedStateObservations.observation: A critical URL-validation pitfall (substring hostname matching for domain allowlists) was missed in implementation/tests; adding this to shared guidance would prevent repeats in future URL-intake features.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: `lib/domain/links.ts` uses `hostname.includes('douyin.com')`; transcript shows no adversarial-host test coverage and tests in `tests/domain/links.test.ts` only check obvious non-Douyin domains.

summary: Review failed due to a blocking domain-validation bug in link classification. Core routing/session behavior is present, but unsupported hostnames can be wrongly accepted as Douyin links.
